title: fix(governance): put fence enforcement plumbing under CODEOWNERS
intent: CI-RED-021 enforcement prep. PR #196 made CODEOWNERS resolvable by replacing the inert @toolbox-governance owner with @HanzoRazer. This follow-up extends the same owner-routing metadata to the enforcement plumbing itself: .github/CODEOWNERS and .github/workflows/architecture_scan.yml. The actual branch/ruleset settings are still the enforcement mechanism; this PR only makes the CODEOWNERS surface coherent before/while those settings are verified.

diff_articulation.what_changed: (1) .github/CODEOWNERS: add @HanzoRazer ownership for .github/CODEOWNERS and .github/workflows/architecture_scan.yml. Existing governance-path rules remain unchanged. (2) this per-PR CBSP21 manifest.
diff_articulation.why_not_redundant: The fence baselines and architecture scripts were already CODEOWNERS-covered, but the CODEOWNERS file and the workflow that runs the required fence were not. If branch/ruleset enforcement is meant to protect the fence from advisory-only drift, its review-routing metadata should cover the enforcement plumbing too. This is a narrow metadata-only prep change; it does not enable CODEOWNER review by itself and does not close CI-RED-021.

files.intent: Add CODEOWNERS coverage for the CODEOWNERS file and the architecture_scan workflow that reports Fence Checks (Blocking).
Per-PR CBSP21 manifest declaring this narrow CODEOWNERS surface expansion.
